George McTurnan Kahin papers, 1945-2000
Collection Number: 14-27-3146

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
George McTurnan Kahin papers, 1945-2000
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
14-27-3146
Abstract:
Correspondence, Cornell lectures and course materials, articles and speeches, files about meetings, and book draft.
Creator:
Kahin, George McTurnan.
Quanitities:
93.1 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Professor of International Studies. George McTurnan Kahin graduated from Harvard University in 1940 and completed graduate work at Stanford University and Johns Hopkins University. He served in the Army during World War II, and lived in Indonesia from 1948-1949, during the peak of Indonesia's war for independence. He came to Cornell in 1951 as an assistant professor of government and executive director of the newly formed Southeast Asia Program. He served as director of the program until 1970, and also founded the Cornell Modern Indonesia Project in 1954, and directed it until his retirement in 1988.

INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

George McTurnan Kahin Papers, #14-27-3146. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content

Correspondence, Cornell lectures and course materials, articles and speeches, files about meetings, book draft, annotated copies of books on the Vietnam War.
Access Restrictions:

Access restricted to the permission of donor until 2026.
Boxes 80-93 are closed until processed.
Related Materials

See also George McTurnan Kahin Lectures, #37-7-527 and George McTurnan Kahin collection on the origins of the Vietnam war, #4251.

CONTAINER LIST
Container
Description
Date
Series I. Publications and speeches
Scope and Contents
Drafts, outlines, and reviews of books and essays written by Kahin. Also included are articles on Kahin and reviews he wrote on the works of others.
